reviews.review_text: look, they just updated it again and its like 11 years old. at i am at 3292 hours. so yeah its great

As my first Total War game, I initially thought Total War: Rome II was amazing. The more I played, though, the more I became frustrated with its systems. Flanking and outmaneuvering barely matter because stats are king most of the time. The political system is absolutely terrible — it feels less like a strategic mechanic and more like something designed purely to make the game less fun.

Everything in the political system works against you. There is almost no point in maintaining armies or relationships with political parties because they are never satisfied. Even when your political party supports you, sending them on diplomatic missions has an absurdly high chance of backfiring. They constantly steal influence, making you poorer and reducing your control. Without exaggeration, this is one of the worst systems I have seen in a game — second only to the “lose all your stuff when you die” mechanic in Souls-like games.

Diplomacy between factions is not much better. The entire campaign feels designed to screw the player over. Allies drag you into endless wars, while satrapies and client states are constantly attacked because they are weak and everyone wants their land. They barely provide anything useful in return. On top of that, the AI will randomly declare war on you even when relations are very positive.

Public order is absurdly punishing, and until the late game there is never enough money to properly manage armies, fleets, agents, and city development all at once — even when you are playing well. The whole campaign balance feels like a complete mess.

What really convinced me this game does not work properly is that I have played other Total War games that I genuinely enjoyed, such as Total War: Shogun 2, Medieval II: Total War, Rome: Total War, and even a bit of A Total War Saga: Troy. Those games felt more focused and enjoyable. In comparison, Rome II becomes an exhausting slog.

Playing on lower difficulties makes the AI too passive and slow, while higher difficulties become overly demanding and frustrating instead of challenging. I played four campaigns on Legendary and managed to do well, but the experience eventually became unbearable.

Overall, I can see why people enjoy Rome II, but for me the campaign systems and balancing issues completely ruined what could have been a great strategy game.

Total War Rome II is great. The variety of military units, bonuses, and city development make the game re-playable with a different outcome every time. I like how everything is going against the player as it keeps the campaign fun. You may think your strong ally is your ally and then they turn around and declare war on you. You now have to pivot and decide whether to pull your forces back and fight your new enemy or (my favorite) fight a two front war and see the chaos. I highly recommend the game as it is endless fun. Adding mods to the game will just be a cherry on top.

I really wanted to enjoy rome 2, but I've had too many issues. My game frequently freezes when saving and sometimes when entering battles, depsite other games running perfectly fine on the same PC. 

The other major issue is the delay in unit responsiveness. Orders often take a noticeable amount of time to register, which is incredibly frustrating in a stratergy game where timing matters. I already dislike the input delay in multiplayer games like Age Of Empires IV, but Rome 2 feels even worse at times. 

The setting and scale are fantastic, but the freezes and sluggish unit response make it hard for me to enjoy the game.

As these strategy games go, Rome II is a good one. If you liked the other titles from the Total War series, it may be a bit too familiar, but it's got good art and is fun to discover. Get it on a super sale.

Very limited gameplay, campaign wise without any DLC's. The game is obviously a little choppy and has some bugs and crashes, gameplay and battling is very fun and takes a long time to master. Overall 7-8/10 game

Time has redeemed this Total War, especially in comparison to newer releases.

Frankly, this *is* a step back from Medieval 2, Rome 1, Shogun 2 Total Wars, however it retains enough of the depth that later games abandon to keep this a fun game. Naturally, a poor DLC policy but inside is a game that challenges you both on the battle map and the campaign map while keeping the fun potential alive in both aspects.

Looks good too, and has a good modding scene that's still alive to add factions + depth wherever you want. I would recommend this easily around $10, a sale around $15-20 if you're interested in other Total Wars and want something around the Roman Times but don't want to play Rome 1.
Tons of content, great unit and faction diversity, tons of mods, and runs well on my older pc.  Been playing off and on since launch, always come back for a campaign.
Not as good as Rome I, get still holds up with better graphics
very fun game would suggest to others intrested in acient history
A great classic that had a rough lauch but is still really fun in 2026. Vanilla is good but especially with overhaul mods like Divide et Impera this game shines.
A Great strategy game and historical background.

How can I start a review for the fans of ancient history and total war... hmm

Wanna commit warcrimes and take over Alexanders dream? Play this game, and see that battles are rather better than the other games. Animations absolutely suck, but we are here for the gameplay, eh? You've got in the base game, grand campaign and the rest of the states, which btw they are peak, all of the base ones. I usually recommend new players to game on a small nation, so they are freer and they start looking for the whole things such as the army, political focuses, and investigation, sth that for example, if you're rome its quite peak, but for the "barbarian nations", you will relealise in the military side its worth picking some and then just move onto civilian. Its a game that requires lots of patience, and say whether is worth continuing  100 turn game or say ♥♥♥♥ it restart. 

If you've played HOI4, its quite similar when it comes to pushes, when to fight battles, when to retreat and make peace, that kind of stuff. Its not like total war Empire where you just have levels and wipe out your enemies, when its late game its a small but steadily grind, thanks to that patience. Now the political area its quite interesting, just try to get all of the factios happy if you dont want a massive civil war during late game, that will totally ♥♥♥♥ up your game. If you win tho, your faction and the rest will earn massive buffs. Now, the other dlcs are quite interesting altho my 2 favourite are caesar in gaul, and rise of the republic, the rest are not that great, dont get me wrong, but I just rather those 2. Now, for the interesting part, research you've civilian area and military area. Depends on the culture, lets say hellene you will have certain stuff that latin (rome) doesnt. And certain factions such as Syracuse doesnt have the same than for example Pergamun. 

Its quite a nice game, dont care about graphs and animations tho, but the battles will be peak, there are bugs,  but nothing crazy when it comes to fighting battles. Just goofy stuff. The mods are good too but try it out some hours without mods and then try if you wanna indeed. Enjoy!

After 1800 Hours I'd say it's pretty good. Runs better than Attila but not a better game than Attila in my opinion. I don't like the Unit cards, I understand that using Hieroglyphics brings a more Ancient feel but I personally like to see my units armor and more detail mainly in the unit card. Also the AI is worse than Attila in my opinion. Worth the money for sure. If people are complaining they suck at the game and if you challenge me on that I will destroy you in multiplayer lol.
the total war series is the ultimate strategy game in my opinion while computer ai is repetitive and at times moronic the game still achieves what few other games can in depth and time invested.

It's old, and the AI do some strange things sometimes, but the amount of variety and ways you can play the campaigns and some of the DLCs are really good make this a pretty fun total war game. I recommend playing on hard difficulty or above as there was little challenge on normal.

My review has a caveat in that i've only played this game using the Divide et Impera mod - nonetheless its a classic strategy game and its been popular for so many years for a reason. The fact that the modding community is still so active is a testament to that.
Its a fun game with ez to learn mechanics that a child could learn and you can run on a laptop like me

Such a joy to play this game. I play this with my dad every week and we love it so much. The game is very much worth your time and money. I heavily reccomend at looking at the DLC's because they are all really good for the most part.

You MUST play this with Divide Et Impera overhaul mod. You are not fully experiencing Rome II without it

Ancient Empires for Total War Atilla is also worth a try if you are interested in this time period

Let me just say, this game is one of the best ive played. Back on my old account, I had about 589 hrs on it. This game has countless DLC's to keep the fun going and the hundreds of hrs ive spent of the online battle mode is unreal, there is still a very strong community from this game on or offline.

GAMEPLAY:
The main campaign is really fun, it is not too hard, but not to easy. You have to manage an empire, not an easy task; the game dives in really deep with managing a population, your finances, legions, roman politics, civil wars, and so much more that this reveiw would be like 20 paragraphs long.
Would highly reccomend this game to roman history fans no doubt.

LEARNING CURVE: 
The game can either take a few days to a few months to learn. If you are not good with roman history game this game might not be for you because the game has nothing to do with a modern setting; if you walked into this game thinking it would have similar mechanics to other managing games in more recent setting, you would be very lost; this game is about the roman timeline and the roman timeline only.

IS IT WORTH IT?
If you love roman history, managing huge empires, finances, legions and the deep battle mechanics of Roman battles and are willing to acually learn the game, get it. But if you are not good with roman history, and easily get overwhelmed buy complex mechanics, do not get.

most likely the best total war out there today, dei makes it truly enjoyable to play. i would say medival 2 is better which in many ways it is but with the graphics i just cant say that aswell as the original rome total war. 100% top 5 total war games
Love this game! It's one of the classic Total War games that walks us through the Roman period.

Absolute peak. Nothing beats conquering Gaul for the glory of Rome, only to have a rival faction secede with several of your strongest legions and settlements whilst the fires of rebellion are igniting across your once beautiful empire.

Played many hours alone and with friends. This has been a staple of fun. Only thing I would change is the later addition of SJW elements into the franchise that has never been part of historicity that the games originally strived for. Lots of faction and campaigns and played the game during release be for the emperor update. They are still supporting the game via updates after years of its release.

Whew. Marking this as a not recommend is hard. But this isn't nearly as polished as other total wars. There are not events, quests, whatever you'd like to call them. Historical battles are EXTREMELY limited without DLC, and each is overpriced and adds one or two. 

There is only one main, "Grand Campaign". Its victory conditions are absurd, requiring you to take nearly the entirety of the map, every single province. And, when you get close to this goal, without warning, the game declares civil war no matter how stable your politics and populace. When it does this, it randomly takes your territory, your armies, all of it. There is no, win a battle and reclaim it, no negotiations. Over half your territory, your military, is now a MASSIVE AI faction that is completely hostile to you. This is not good game design. It is flagrant BS. Most of the DLC is awful, just slight reframing of the Grand Campaign, slight narrowing of scope, restrictions on the tech tree, none of it really letting you play late game and all restricting to early. This game has all the mechanics to be wonderful. Graphics, battles, territory management, and diplomacy. They BUILT a great game. Then implemented it in the single worst possible way.
Played 1263 hours, great game. Civil wars suck and the map is to small. 9.5 out of 10. Love historical total war games.
